skill.name: AI Vision Analysis — verging.ai
skill.description: Analyze images using GPT-4o Vision through the verging.ai proxy API. Supports both image URL and file upload.
skill.summary: Analyze images with GPT-4o Vision via verging.ai. Extract descriptions, detect objects, and read text from images.
skill.author: verging-ai

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation file for using verging.ai's GPT-4o Vision API. The static analyzer flagged 19 patterns, but all are false positives caused by scanning markdown code examples. The 'external_commands' findings are bash code blocks in documentation (npx, export, curl). The 'network' findings are documentation references to verging.ai API endpoints. The 'env_access' findings show standard API key authentication patterns. No executable code exists in this skill; it only provides instructions for users to make API calls themselves.

low_findings.title: Documentation contains example shell commands
low_findings.description: README.md contains bash code blocks showing npx install, export, and curl commands. These are documentation examples for users, not executable code in the skill. The static scanner flagged the backtick code blocks as potential command execution. No actual code execution occurs; the skill is a documentation file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed false positive: the skill contains only markdown documentation. The flagged patterns are bash code examples inside markdown code blocks that users would manually run.

low_findings.title: API key required in environment variable
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to set VERGING_API_KEY as an environment variable for authentication with verging.ai. This is a standard API authentication pattern. The static scanner flagged the 'export VERGING_API_KEY' and '$VERGING_API_KEY' references as credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard API key authentication pattern. Users set their own key; the skill does not read, exfiltrate, or manipulate the key.

low_findings.title: Network calls to third-party API endpoints
low_findings.description: Documentation references verging.ai API endpoints (https://verging.ai/api/v1/ai/vision and /vision/upload). The static scanner flagged these as hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate API endpoints for the vision analysis service the skill is designed to use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive: the URLs are API endpoints users call to use the vision service. The skill itself does not execute network requests; it documents how users should make them.

content.value_statement: Extract descriptions, objects, and text from images using AI vision capabilities. This skill provides a simple API integration for GPT-4o Vision through verging.ai.
actual_capabilities: Analyze images by providing a URL (JSON API mode)
Analyze local image files via multipart upload
Extract detailed scene descriptions from images
Detect and identify objects within images
Configure response length using max_tokens parameter
limitations: Requires a verging.ai account and valid API key
Minimum 5 credits balance required before making requests
URL mode and file upload use separate API endpoints
Credit-based pricing: 2 credits base fee plus token usage

use_cases.title: Generate alt text for website images
use_cases.description: Content creators and web developers can use this skill to automatically generate descriptive alt text for images on their websites, improving accessibility.

use_cases.title: Integrate vision AI into applications
use_cases.description: Developers building apps that need image understanding can use this skill's API documentation to quickly integrate GPT-4o Vision capabilities.

use_cases.title: Extract text from document images
use_cases.description: Researchers and office workers can use this skill to extract text from scanned documents, screenshots, or photos containing text.

prompt_templates.title: Basic image description
prompt_templates.prompt: Describe what you see in the image at [URL] in detail.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting a general description of an image

prompt_templates.title: Object detection
prompt_templates.prompt: List all the objects you can identify in this image: [URL]

prompt_templates.title: Text extraction from image
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all the text visible in this image: [URL]. Preserve the formatting and structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: OCR - extracting text from images or scanned documents

prompt_templates.title: Detailed visual analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this image at [URL]. Provide: 1) A detailed scene description, 2) All visible objects, 3) Any text present, 4) The overall mood and context.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive image analysis with multiple output aspects

output_examples.input: A photo of a modern office with people working at desks
output_examples.output: The image shows a modern open-plan office space with approximately 8-10 people working at white desks. There are computer monitors, laptops, and office chairs visible. The lighting is bright with overhead LED panels. Large windows on the left side provide natural light. A small meeting area with a round table is visible in the background.

output_examples.input: A screenshot containing text: 'Meeting at 3pm in Conference Room B'
output_examples.output: Text extracted from image: 'Meeting at 3pm in Conference Room B'. The text appears to be in a standard sans-serif font, white text on a dark blue background.

best_practices: Store your VERGING_API_KEY securely in an environment variable, never hardcode it in scripts
Use the URL mode for images already hosted online to reduce upload time and credit usage
Set an appropriate max_tokens value to control response length and manage credit consumption
anti_patterns: Do not share your API key in public repositories, chat messages, or screenshots
Do not use this skill for processing sensitive or private images without reviewing verging.ai's privacy policy
Do not exceed reasonable request rates; respect rate limits to avoid 429 errors

faq.question: What is verging.ai?
faq.answer: Verging.ai is a third-party API proxy service that provides access to AI models including GPT-4o Vision. This skill uses their API to perform image analysis.

faq.question: Do I need a verging.ai account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to create an account at verging.ai and generate an API key to use this skill. Set the key as the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: What is the minimum credit balance required?
faq.answer: You need a minimum balance of 5 credits before making any API request. Each request costs a base fee of 2 credits plus token usage charges.

faq.question: Can I analyze local image files?
faq.answer: Yes, use the file upload endpoint (https://verging.ai/api/v1/ai/vision/upload) with multipart form data. For images hosted online, use the URL endpoint with a JSON request.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The API supports common image formats including JPEG, PNG, GIF, and WebP. For best results, use clear images with good resolution.

faq.question: How is the response formatted?
faq.answer: The API returns JSON with three fields: content (the analysis text), usage (token counts), and credits_consumed (credits charged for the request).
